data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on OrthoPediatrics, Maintains $23 Price Target on Strong Q1 Beat and Raised Sales Guidance
data.description:
OrthoPediatrics, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Mathew Blackman from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $23.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mathew Blackman has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight both operational strength and attractive valuation. OrthoPediatrics delivered first-quarter revenue and adjusted EBITDA meaningfully above expectations, even after facing weather-related disruptions in its U.S. bracing business, and management modestly lifted full-year sales guidance while reiterating profitability and free cash flow targets.

Blackman also points to robust growth across core franchises, including double-digit gains in Trauma & Deformity, Scoliosis, and Specialty Bracing, supported by strong demand for products like VerteGlide and continued clinic expansion. He views KIDS as a high-quality, under-recognized asset with solid near- and long-term growth prospects, and maintains a $23 price target, which remains unchanged, reflecting further upside potential from current levels.

In another report released on April 24, Canaccord Genuity also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $25.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Phil Nadeau Reiterates Buy on Syndax, Citing Strong Revuforj Momentum, Longer Treatment Durations, and Emerging Niktimvo Upside
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Phil Nadeau has maintained their bullish stance on SNDX stock, giving a Buy rating on April 20.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Phil Nadeau has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Syndax’s strong commercial execution and expansion potential. Revuforj is already generating rapidly growing revenue, supported by rising prescription volumes, high formulary coverage, and increasing use in both KMT2A and the still underpenetrated NPM1 segment, which together point to a durable growth runway.

At the same time, the company is seeing longer treatment durations as more post‑transplant KMT2A patients return for maintenance therapy, enhancing revenue visibility and profitability prospects. Layered onto this is the emerging opportunity from Niktimvo, where underlying demand is building and upcoming proof‑of‑concept data could unlock additional sizable markets, making Syndax a differentiated small‑mid cap biotech with multiple value-creating catalysts ahead.

In another report released on April 20, Barclays also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $35.00 price target.

data.title: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on Dexcom as Margin Strength and Global Patient Growth Offset U.S. CGM Concerns; Price Target Reset to $75
data.description:
Josh Jennings, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Buy rating on Dexcom. The associated price target was lowered to $75.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Josh Jennings has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that signal durable growth and improving profitability at Dexcom. He notes that, despite a modest U.S. revenue shortfall that reinforced bearish worries about a slower domestic CGM market, the company delivered record worldwide new patient starts, sequential U.S. new patient growth, and solid international expansion, supporting confidence in future demand.

Jennings also highlights broad-based margin strength, with gross, operating, and EBITDA margins all topping expectations, helped by manufacturing efficiencies, logistics savings, and leverage in operating expenses. He emphasizes that management raised profitability guidance and maintains conviction that expanded CMS coverage for Type 2 patients not on insulin should reignite U.S. growth, which underpins the Buy rating even as the official price target is reset to $75.

In another report released on April 20, Goldman Sachs also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $82.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
